key_insight: ACE interatomic potentials, trained on an expanded DFT database, can markedly improve screw-dislocation predictions in bcc refractory metals, but success depends strongly on both element and training data composition.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable atomistic screening of refractory metals for high-temperature strength and plasticity, especially where dislocation core behavior and Peierls barriers control flow stress.
method: Extended an existing DFT database for group 5 and 6 refractory metals (V, Nb, Ta, Mo, W).
Trained atomic cluster expansion (ACE) potentials and evaluated screw-dislocation core behavior, glide plane, and Peierls barrier.
Compared dislocation predictions against DFT and used kink-pair models for Nb, Mo, and W.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports near-DFT accuracy for Mo and W, substantial improvement for V, Nb, and Ta, and reliable kink-pair activation enthalpies for Nb, Mo, and W.
watch_item: A single-humped Peierls barrier is not sufficient to validate dislocation-glide fidelity, so database coverage and element-specific transferability must be checked carefully.
paper_title: Atomic Cluster Expansion Potentials for Screw Dislocations in BCC Refractory Metals
